name: Classic Nasi Goreng Kampung
description: Authentic Indonesian fried rice flavored with garlic, shallots, sweet soy sauce, and shrimp paste—perfectly executed in the traditional way.

instructions.description: Heat vegetable oil in a large wok over medium-high heat. Scramble one egg and set aside.
In the same wok, sauté sliced shallots, minced garlic, and chopped chilies until fragrant.
Add the diced chicken and cook until it turns golden brown.
Add the shrimp paste and stir well to break it up.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, then drizzle sweet soy sauce and salty soy sauce over the rice.
Stir-fry vigorously on high heat for 3-5 minutes until the rice is evenly coated and slightly caramelized.
Mix in the scrambled eggs and cook for another minute.
Serve hot, garnished with a sunny-side-up egg, cucumber slices, and shrimp crackers (kerupuk).

name: Spicy Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, hard cheese, cured pork, and black pepper—infused with extra chili and spices for a fiery kick.

ingredients.name: Crushed red pepper flakes or chili powder

instructions.description: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the spaghetti according to package directions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, heat a large pan over medium heat. Render the pancetta or guanciale cubes until crispy and golden. Remove pan from heat. Fold in the extra chili flakes for maximum heat.
In a bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, whole egg, grated Pecorino Romano, and Parmigiano Reggiano cheese. Season generously with crushed black pepper.
Drain the spaghetti, reserving 1 cup of pasta cooking water.
Immediately add the hot pasta to the pan with the pancetta, tossing to coat in the rendered fat.
Let the pan cool slightly for 30 seconds. Pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta, tossing quickly to create a creamy sauce without scrambling the eggs.
Add a splash of reserved pasta water if needed to loosen the sauce to a silky consistency.
Serve immediately with extra grated cheese and black pepper.

name: Vegetarian Sate Ayam Madura
description: Succulent chicken skewers grilled over hot charcoal, served with a rich and savory peanut sauce—made with plant-based ingredients without sacrificing flavor.

ingredients.name: Organic firm tofu or wild mixed mushrooms (diced)

instructions.description: Thread the cubed tofu/mushrooms fillet onto the soaked bamboo skewers, about 3-4 pieces per skewer.
Marinate skewers in a mixture of sweet soy sauce, oil, coriander powder, and lime juice for at least 30 minutes.
For the peanut sauce: Blend peanuts, garlic, shallots, candlenuts, and palm sugar with a little water until smooth.
Transfer peanut mixture to a saucepan. Add salt, sweet soy sauce, and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until oil separates.
Preheat a grill pan or charcoal grill. Brush tofu/mushrooms skewers with oil and sweet soy sauce.
Grill skewers, turning occasionally, until charred and cooked through (about 8-10 minutes).
Serve hot skewers topped with peanut sauce, a drizzle of sweet soy sauce, sliced shallots, and lime wedges.

name: Gourmet Classic Beef Rendang
description: A slow-cooked dry curry of beef, simmered in coconut milk and a complex spice paste until extremely tender and intensely aromatic—elevated with premium ingredients and professional chef secrets.

instructions.description: Blend shallots, garlic, chilies, ginger, galangal, and turmeric with a bit of oil or water into a smooth spice paste.
In a large deep heavy-bottomed pot, combine the spice paste, coconut milk, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, and turmeric leaf. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Add the beef cubes and stir well to coat with the mixture.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces and turns thick and brown (approx. 2 hours).
Add the kerisik (toasted grated coconut) and salt to taste.
Continue cooking on low heat, stirring constantly to prevent burning, until the beef is tender, the liquid has completely evaporated, and the beef is dark brown and oily (approx 1 more hour).
Allow the rendang to rest before serving. It tastes even better the next day!

name: Healthy Pizza Margherita
description: Simple and timeless Neapolitan pizza topped with fresh tomato sauce, creamy mozzarella, and sweet basil leaves—prepared with low-sodium, nutrient-rich ingredients for guilt-free indulgence.

instructions.description: Preheat your oven with a pizza stone inside to the highest possible temperature (usually 250C or 500F) for 1 hour.
Crush San Marzano tomatoes with a pinch of salt to make a simple sauce.
Stretch the pizza dough on a surface dusted with semolina flour into a 12-inch circle.
Transfer stretched dough to a pizza peel.
Spread tomato sauce evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border.
Distribute fresh mozzarella slices evenly over the sauce.
Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and slide onto the preheated pizza stone.
Bake for 7-10 minutes until the crust is puffed and charred, and cheese is bubbly.
Garnish immediately with fresh basil leaves, slice, and serve hot.

name: Homestyle Classic Tacos Al Pastor
description: Flavorful spit-roasted pork marinated in dried chilies, spices, and pineapple, served in warm corn tortillas—comfort food made simple, just like grandma used to make.

instructions.description: Blend achiote paste, guajillo chilies, chipotle, pineapple juice, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and cumin until smooth.
Coat pork slices thoroughly in marinade, cover, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (overnight preferred).
In a hot skillet or griddle, sear marinated pork slices in batches until nicely browned and slightly charred. Chop into bite-sized pieces.
Grill or pan-sear fresh pineapple slices until caramelized, then dice.
Warm corn tortillas on a dry griddle.
Assemble tacos by placing chopped pork in warm tortillas.
Top with diced pineapple, chopped white onion, cilantro, and serve with lime wedges.

name: Quick Traditional Sushi Roll
description: Classic Maki Sushi featuring seasoned sushi rice, fresh cucumber, avocado, and premium raw salmon wrapped in crisp Nori seaweed sheets—optimized for busy weekdays, ready in a flash.

instructions.description: Rinse sushi rice thoroughly until water runs clear. Cook in a rice cooker with slightly less water than normal.
Mix rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Drizzle over hot cooked rice, gently folding with a wooden paddle. Let cool to room temp.
Cover a bamboo sushi rolling mat with plastic wrap.
Place a nori sheet, shiny side down, on the mat. Spread a thin layer of cooled sushi rice evenly over the nori, leaving a 1-inch border at the top.
Arrange salmon slices, cucumber sticks, and avocado slices in a horizontal line across the center of the rice.
Roll the mat forward tightly, tucking the ingredients in, until the rice edges meet. Seal the nori edge with a drop of water.
Using a very sharp, wet knife, slice the roll into 8 equal pieces.
Serve chilled with soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.

name: Smoky BBQ Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender marinated chicken chunks grilled to perfection and then simmered in a rich, creamy, spiced tomato sauce—infused with rich hickory wood smoke and bold flavorings.

instructions.description: Marinate chicken in yogurt, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, cumin, chili powder, and salt for 1 hour.
Preheat oven broiler or grill. Thread chicken onto skewers and cook for 10-12 minutes until charred and cooked through.
In a large pan, melt butter over medium heat. Sauté chopped onions until golden brown.
Stir in ginger-garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin, and tomato puree. Cook for 10 minutes until sauce darkens.
Add the grilled chicken tikka pieces to the sauce. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook for a final 2 minutes. Do not boil.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot with garlic naan or basmati rice.

name: Garlic Butter Gourmet Cheeseburger
description: A juicy prime beef patty grilled to medium-rare, topped with melted sharp cheddar, caramelized onions, and house burger sauce on a brioche bun—richly bathed in an aromatic, sizzling garlic-butter blend.

instructions.description: Caramelize the sliced onions in butter over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until dark brown and sweet.
Divide ground beef into two portions. Shape into flat, round patties slightly larger than the buns. Season both sides aggressively with salt and pepper.
Combine mayonnaise, ketchup, and Dijon mustard in a small bowl to make the house burger sauce.
Preheat a cast-iron skillet on high heat until smoking. Toast brioche bun halves face down in dry skillet for 1 minute.
Sear patties for 2-3 minutes. Flip, immediately top with cheddar slices, and cover with a lid to melt cheese for another 2 minutes.
Spread burger sauce on both bun halves.
Assemble by placing lettuce and tomato on bottom bun, followed by beef patty, cheese, caramelized onions, and top bun.
Serve immediately with french fries.

name: Traditional Tom Yum Goong
description: An iconic Thai hot and sour soup with succulent prawns, fresh mushrooms, lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves—honoring historical preparation methods for an authentic taste profile.

instructions.description: In a large pot, bring chicken or shrimp stock to a boil.
Add lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, and bruised chilies. Simmer for 5 minutes until stock is highly aromatic.
Stir in the Thai chili paste, mushrooms, and fish sauce.
Add the prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes just until they turn pink and opaque.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the lime juice (adding it while boiling can make it bitter).
Taste and adjust sourness and spiciness. Serve hot garnished with cilantro leaves.

name: Classic Falafel & Hummus
description: Crispy, herb-packed chickpea fritters served alongside a smooth, velvety tahini hummus—perfectly executed in the traditional way.

instructions.description: For falafel: Drain soaked chickpeas. Process in a food processor with herbs, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper until finely ground but not pureed.
Transfer falafel mixture to a bowl, stir in baking powder, cover, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
For hummus: Blend canned chickpeas, tahini, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and ice-cold water until incredibly smooth and creamy. Drizzle with olive oil.
Shape falafel mixture into small balls or patties.
Deep fry in hot vegetable oil at 180C (350F) for 3-4 minutes until dark golden and crispy.
Spread hummus onto a serving platter, top with warm falafels, a sprinkle of paprika, and serve with warm pita bread.

name: Spicy French Onion Soup
description: Rich, deeply caramelized onion broth flavored with sherry and thyme, topped with toasted baguette slices and melted Gruyère cheese—infused with extra chili and spices for a fiery kick.

ingredients.name: Crushed red pepper flakes or chili powder

instructions.description: Melt butter in a large heavy pot. Add onions and cook on medium-low heat, stirring frequently, for 45-50 minutes until sweet and caramelized dark brown.
Deglaze the pot with white wine, scraping up all the browned bits from the bottom. Fold in the extra chili flakes for maximum heat.
Add the beef stock, thyme sprigs, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 20 minutes.
Preheat oven broiler.
Toast baguette slices until dry and golden.
Ladle hot soup into oven-safe soup bowls. Discard thyme and bay leaf.
Place 1-2 baguette slices on top of each bowl and cover generously with grated Gruyère cheese.
Broil for 3-4 minutes until cheese is melted, bubbly, and beautifully browned. Serve immediately.

name: Vegetarian Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Chewy in the center, crispy on the edges, packed with premium dark chocolate chips and a hint of sea salt—made with plant-based ingredients without sacrificing flavor.

instructions.description: In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk melted butter, brown sugar, and white sugar until completely combined and smooth.
Whisk in the eggs one at a time, followed by vanilla extract, until mixture is pale and slightly thickened.
Fold in the flour mixture gently with a spatula just until combined (do not overmix).
Fold in the dark chocolate chips.
Chill the cookie dough in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 175C (350F). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Scoop 2-tablespoon-sized balls of dough, place 2 inches apart on sheets.
Bake for 9-11 minutes until edges are golden brown but centers are soft.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt immediately, cool on sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.

name: Gourmet Guacamole & Pico de Gallo
description: Fresh, vibrant avocado dip alongside a crisp, zesty tomato-onion salsa, served with salted tortilla chips—elevated with premium ingredients and professional chef secrets.

instructions.description: For Pico de Gallo: In a bowl, combine diced tomatoes, half of the chopped onion, half of the cilantro, 1 diced jalapeño, juice of 1 lime, and salt. Stir and let sit to marinate.
For Guacamole: Slice avocados, remove pits, and scoop flesh into a large bowl.
Add remaining red onion, cilantro, diced jalapeño, minced garlic, juice of 2 limes, and salt.
Mash with a fork or potato masher to desired consistency (some chunks are good!).
Taste and adjust salt or lime juice if necessary.
Serve the fresh guacamole and salsa pico de gallo side-by-side with crisp tortilla chips.

name: Healthy Premium Matcha Latte
description: Smooth and earthy Japanese ceremonial-grade green tea whisked with creamy steamed milk and a touch of honey—prepared with low-sodium, nutrient-rich ingredients for guilt-free indulgence.

instructions.description: Sift matcha powder through a fine-mesh strainer into a wide tea bowl (chawan) to remove clumps.
Add the hot water (make sure it is not boiling, around 80C, or it will burn the matcha).
Use a bamboo whisk (chasen) to whisk vigorously in a 'W' or 'M' pattern until a frothy layer of fine bubbles forms on top (approx. 1 minute).
In a separate saucepan or milk frother, heat and froth the milk until steamy and velvety.
Stir honey or sweetener into the whisked matcha.
Pour the warm matcha into your serving mug, then slowly pour the steamed frothed milk on top.
Dust with a tiny pinch of matcha powder for presentation.

Preheat a grill pan or charcoal grill. Brush tofu/mushrooms skewers with oil and sweet soy sauce.
Grill skewers, turning occasionally, until charred and cooked through (about 8-10 minutes).
Serve hot skewers topped with peanut sauce, a drizzle of sweet soy sauce, sliced shallots, and lime wedges.

name: Gourmet Classic Beef Rendang
description: A slow-cooked dry curry of beef, simmered in coconut milk and a complex spice paste until extremely tender and intensely aromatic—elevated with premium ingredients and professional chef secrets.

instructions.description: Blend shallots, garlic, chilies, ginger, galangal, and turmeric with a bit of oil or water into a smooth spice paste.
In a large deep heavy-bottomed pot, combine the spice paste, coconut milk, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, and turmeric leaf. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Add the beef cubes and stir well to coat with the mixture.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces and turns thick and brown (approx. 2 hours).
Add the kerisik (toasted grated coconut) and salt to taste.
Continue cooking on low heat, stirring constantly to prevent burning, until the beef is tender, the liquid has completely evaporated, and the beef is dark brown and oily (approx 1 more hour).
Allow the rendang to rest before serving. It tastes even better the next day!

name: Healthy Pizza Margherita
description: Simple and timeless Neapolitan pizza topped with fresh tomato sauce, creamy mozzarella, and sweet basil leaves—prepared with low-sodium, nutrient-rich ingredients for guilt-free indulgence.

instructions.description: Preheat your oven with a pizza stone inside to the highest possible temperature (usually 250C or 500F) for 1 hour.
Crush San Marzano tomatoes with a pinch of salt to make a simple sauce.
Stretch the pizza dough on a surface dusted with semolina flour into a 12-inch circle.
Transfer stretched dough to a pizza peel.
Spread tomato sauce evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border.
Distribute fresh mozzarella slices evenly over the sauce.
Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and slide onto the preheated pizza stone.
Bake for 7-10 minutes until the crust is puffed and charred, and cheese is bubbly.
Garnish immediately with fresh basil leaves, slice, and serve hot.

name: Homestyle Classic Tacos Al Pastor
description: Flavorful spit-roasted pork marinated in dried chilies, spices, and pineapple, served in warm corn tortillas—comfort food made simple, just like grandma used to make.

instructions.description: Blend achiote paste, guajillo chilies, chipotle, pineapple juice, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and cumin until smooth.
Coat pork slices thoroughly in marinade, cover, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (overnight preferred).
In a hot skillet or griddle, sear marinated pork slices in batches until nicely browned and slightly charred. Chop into bite-sized pieces.
Grill or pan-sear fresh pineapple slices until caramelized, then dice.
Warm corn tortillas on a dry griddle.
Assemble tacos by placing chopped pork in warm tortillas.
Top with diced pineapple, chopped white onion, cilantro, and serve with lime wedges.

name: Quick Traditional Sushi Roll
description: Classic Maki Sushi featuring seasoned sushi rice, fresh cucumber, avocado, and premium raw salmon wrapped in crisp Nori seaweed sheets—optimized for busy weekdays, ready in a flash.

instructions.description: Rinse sushi rice thoroughly until water runs clear. Cook in a rice cooker with slightly less water than normal.
Mix rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Drizzle over hot cooked rice, gently folding with a wooden paddle. Let cool to room temp.
Cover a bamboo sushi rolling mat with plastic wrap.
Place a nori sheet, shiny side down, on the mat. Spread a thin layer of cooled sushi rice evenly over the nori, leaving a 1-inch border at the top.
Arrange salmon slices, cucumber sticks, and avocado slices in a horizontal line across the center of the rice.
Roll the mat forward tightly, tucking the ingredients in, until the rice edges meet. Seal the nori edge with a drop of water.
Using a very sharp, wet knife, slice the roll into 8 equal pieces.
Serve chilled with soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.

name: Smoky BBQ Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender marinated chicken chunks grilled to perfection and then simmered in a rich, creamy, spiced tomato sauce—infused with rich hickory wood smoke and bold flavorings.

instructions.description: Marinate chicken in yogurt, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, cumin, chili powder, and salt for 1 hour.
Preheat oven broiler or grill. Thread chicken onto skewers and cook for 10-12 minutes until charred and cooked through.
In a large pan, melt butter over medium heat. Sauté chopped onions until golden brown.
Stir in ginger-garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin, and tomato puree. Cook for 10 minutes until sauce darkens.
Add the grilled chicken tikka pieces to the sauce. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook for a final 2 minutes. Do not boil.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot with garlic naan or basmati rice.

name: Garlic Butter Gourmet Cheeseburger
description: A juicy prime beef patty grilled to medium-rare, topped with melted sharp cheddar, caramelized onions, and house burger sauce on a brioche bun—richly bathed in an aromatic, sizzling garlic-butter blend.

instructions.description: Caramelize the sliced onions in butter over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until dark brown and sweet.
Divide ground beef into two portions. Shape into flat, round patties slightly larger than the buns. Season both sides aggressively with salt and pepper.
Combine mayonnaise, ketchup, and Dijon mustard in a small bowl to make the house burger sauce.
Preheat a cast-iron skillet on high heat until smoking. Toast brioche bun halves face down in dry skillet for 1 minute.
Sear patties for 2-3 minutes. Flip, immediately top with cheddar slices, and cover with a lid to melt cheese for another 2 minutes.
Spread burger sauce on both bun halves.
Assemble by placing lettuce and tomato on bottom bun, followed by beef patty, cheese, caramelized onions, and top bun.
Serve immediately with french fries.

name: Traditional Tom Yum Goong
description: An iconic Thai hot and sour soup with succulent prawns, fresh mushrooms, lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves—honoring historical preparation methods for an authentic taste profile.

instructions.description: In a large pot, bring chicken or shrimp stock to a boil.
Add lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, and bruised chilies. Simmer for 5 minutes until stock is highly aromatic.
Stir in the Thai chili paste, mushrooms, and fish sauce.
Add the prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes just until they turn pink and opaque.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the lime juice (adding it while boiling can make it bitter).
Taste and adjust sourness and spiciness. Serve hot garnished with cilantro leaves.

name: Classic Falafel & Hummus
description: Crispy, herb-packed chickpea fritters served alongside a smooth, velvety tahini hummus—perfectly executed in the traditional way.

instructions.description: For falafel: Drain soaked chickpeas. Process in a food processor with herbs, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper until finely ground but not pureed.
Transfer falafel mixture to a bowl, stir in baking powder, cover, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
For hummus: Blend canned chickpeas, tahini, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and ice-cold water until incredibly smooth and creamy. Drizzle with olive oil.
Shape falafel mixture into small balls or patties.
Deep fry in hot vegetable oil at 180C (350F) for 3-4 minutes until dark golden and crispy.
Spread hummus onto a serving platter, top with warm falafels, a sprinkle of paprika, and serve with warm pita bread.

name: Spicy French Onion Soup
description: Rich, deeply caramelized onion broth flavored with sherry and thyme, topped with toasted baguette slices and melted Gruyère cheese—infused with extra chili and spices for a fiery kick.

ingredients.name: Crushed red pepper flakes or chili powder

instructions.description: Melt butter in a large heavy pot. Add onions and cook on medium-low heat, stirring frequently, for 45-50 minutes until sweet and caramelized dark brown.
Deglaze the pot with white wine, scraping up all the browned bits from the bottom. Fold in the extra chili flakes for maximum heat.
Add the beef stock, thyme sprigs, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 20 minutes.
Preheat oven broiler.
Toast baguette slices until dry and golden.
Ladle hot soup into oven-safe soup bowls. Discard thyme and bay leaf.
Place 1-2 baguette slices on top of each bowl and cover generously with grated Gruyère cheese.
Broil for 3-4 minutes until cheese is melted, bubbly, and beautifully browned. Serve immediately.

name: Vegetarian Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Chewy in the center, crispy on the edges, packed with premium dark chocolate chips and a hint of sea salt—made with plant-based ingredients without sacrificing flavor.

instructions.description: In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk melted butter, brown sugar, and white sugar until completely combined and smooth.
Whisk in the eggs one at a time, followed by vanilla extract, until mixture is pale and slightly thickened.
Fold in the flour mixture gently with a spatula just until combined (do not overmix).
Fold in the dark chocolate chips.
Chill the cookie dough in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 175C (350F). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Scoop 2-tablespoon-sized balls of dough, place 2 inches apart on sheets.
Bake for 9-11 minutes until edges are golden brown but centers are soft.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt immediately, cool on sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.

name: Gourmet Guacamole & Pico de Gallo
description: Fresh, vibrant avocado dip alongside a crisp, zesty tomato-onion salsa, served with salted tortilla chips—elevated with premium ingredients and professional chef secrets.

instructions.description: For Pico de Gallo: In a bowl, combine diced tomatoes, half of the chopped onion, half of the cilantro, 1 diced jalapeño, juice of 1 lime, and salt. Stir and let sit to marinate.
For Guacamole: Slice avocados, remove pits, and scoop flesh into a large bowl.
Add remaining red onion, cilantro, diced jalapeño, minced garlic, juice of 2 limes, and salt.
Mash with a fork or potato masher to desired consistency (some chunks are good!).
Taste and adjust salt or lime juice if necessary.
Serve the fresh guacamole and salsa pico de gallo side-by-side with crisp tortilla chips.

name: Healthy Premium Matcha Latte
description: Smooth and earthy Japanese ceremonial-grade green tea whisked with creamy steamed milk and a touch of honey—prepared with low-sodium, nutrient-rich ingredients for guilt-free indulgence.

instructions.description: Sift matcha powder through a fine-mesh strainer into a wide tea bowl (chawan) to remove clumps.
Add the hot water (make sure it is not boiling, around 80C, or it will burn the matcha).
Use a bamboo whisk (chasen) to whisk vigorously in a 'W' or 'M' pattern until a frothy layer of fine bubbles forms on top (approx. 1 minute).
In a separate saucepan or milk frother, heat and froth the milk until steamy and velvety.
Stir honey or sweetener into the whisked matcha.
Pour the warm matcha into your serving mug, then slowly pour the steamed frothed milk on top.
Dust with a tiny pinch of matcha powder for presentation.

name: Homestyle Nasi Goreng Kampung
description: Authentic Indonesian fried rice flavored with garlic, shallots, sweet soy sauce, and shrimp paste—comfort food made simple, just like grandma used to make.

instructions.description: Heat vegetable oil in a large wok over medium-high heat. Scramble one egg and set aside.
In the same wok, sauté sliced shallots, minced garlic, and chopped chilies until fragrant.
Add the diced chicken and cook until it turns golden brown.
Add the shrimp paste and stir well to break it up.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, then drizzle sweet soy sauce and salty soy sauce over the rice.
Stir-fry vigorously on high heat for 3-5 minutes until the rice is evenly coated and slightly caramelized.
Mix in the scrambled eggs and cook for another minute.
Serve hot, garnished with a sunny-side-up egg, cucumber slices, and shrimp crackers (kerupuk).

name: Quick Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, hard cheese, cured pork, and black pepper—optimized for busy weekdays, ready in a flash.

instructions.description: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the spaghetti according to package directions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, heat a large pan over medium heat. Render the pancetta or guanciale cubes until crispy and golden. Remove pan from heat.
In a bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, whole egg, grated Pecorino Romano, and Parmigiano Reggiano cheese. Season generously with crushed black pepper.
Drain the spaghetti, reserving 1 cup of pasta cooking water.
Immediately add the hot pasta to the pan with the pancetta, tossing to coat in the rendered fat.
Let the pan cool slightly for 30 seconds. Pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta, tossing quickly to create a creamy sauce without scrambling the eggs.
Add a splash of reserved pasta water if needed to loosen the sauce to a silky consistency.
Serve immediately with extra grated cheese and black pepper.

instructions.description: Blend achiote paste, guajillo chilies, chipotle, pineapple juice, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and cumin until smooth.
Coat pork slices thoroughly in marinade, cover, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (overnight preferred).
In a hot skillet or griddle, sear marinated pork slices in batches until nicely browned and slightly charred. Chop into bite-sized pieces.
Grill or pan-sear fresh pineapple slices until caramelized, then dice.
Warm corn tortillas on a dry griddle.
Assemble tacos by placing chopped pork in warm tortillas.
Top with diced pineapple, chopped white onion, cilantro, and serve with lime wedges.

name: Quick Traditional Sushi Roll
description: Classic Maki Sushi featuring seasoned sushi rice, fresh cucumber, avocado, and premium raw salmon wrapped in crisp Nori seaweed sheets—optimized for busy weekdays, ready in a flash.

instructions.description: Rinse sushi rice thoroughly until water runs clear. Cook in a rice cooker with slightly less water than normal.
Mix rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Drizzle over hot cooked rice, gently folding with a wooden paddle. Let cool to room temp.
Cover a bamboo sushi rolling mat with plastic wrap.
Place a nori sheet, shiny side down, on the mat. Spread a thin layer of cooled sushi rice evenly over the nori, leaving a 1-inch border at the top.
Arrange salmon slices, cucumber sticks, and avocado slices in a horizontal line across the center of the rice.
Roll the mat forward tightly, tucking the ingredients in, until the rice edges meet. Seal the nori edge with a drop of water.
Using a very sharp, wet knife, slice the roll into 8 equal pieces.
Serve chilled with soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.

name: Smoky BBQ Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender marinated chicken chunks grilled to perfection and then simmered in a rich, creamy, spiced tomato sauce—infused with rich hickory wood smoke and bold flavorings.

instructions.description: Marinate chicken in yogurt, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, cumin, chili powder, and salt for 1 hour.
Preheat oven broiler or grill. Thread chicken onto skewers and cook for 10-12 minutes until charred and cooked through.
In a large pan, melt butter over medium heat. Sauté chopped onions until golden brown.
Stir in ginger-garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin, and tomato puree. Cook for 10 minutes until sauce darkens.
Add the grilled chicken tikka pieces to the sauce. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook for a final 2 minutes. Do not boil.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot with garlic naan or basmati rice.

name: Garlic Butter Gourmet Cheeseburger
description: A juicy prime beef patty grilled to medium-rare, topped with melted sharp cheddar, caramelized onions, and house burger sauce on a brioche bun—richly bathed in an aromatic, sizzling garlic-butter blend.

instructions.description: Caramelize the sliced onions in butter over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until dark brown and sweet.
Divide ground beef into two portions. Shape into flat, round patties slightly larger than the buns. Season both sides aggressively with salt and pepper.
Combine mayonnaise, ketchup, and Dijon mustard in a small bowl to make the house burger sauce.
Preheat a cast-iron skillet on high heat until smoking. Toast brioche bun halves face down in dry skillet for 1 minute.
Sear patties for 2-3 minutes. Flip, immediately top with cheddar slices, and cover with a lid to melt cheese for another 2 minutes.
Spread burger sauce on both bun halves.
Assemble by placing lettuce and tomato on bottom bun, followed by beef patty, cheese, caramelized onions, and top bun.
Serve immediately with french fries.

name: Traditional Tom Yum Goong
description: An iconic Thai hot and sour soup with succulent prawns, fresh mushrooms, lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves—honoring historical preparation methods for an authentic taste profile.

instructions.description: In a large pot, bring chicken or shrimp stock to a boil.
Add lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, and bruised chilies. Simmer for 5 minutes until stock is highly aromatic.
Stir in the Thai chili paste, mushrooms, and fish sauce.
Add the prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes just until they turn pink and opaque.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the lime juice (adding it while boiling can make it bitter).
Taste and adjust sourness and spiciness. Serve hot garnished with cilantro leaves.

name: Classic Falafel & Hummus
description: Crispy, herb-packed chickpea fritters served alongside a smooth, velvety tahini hummus—perfectly executed in the traditional way.

instructions.description: For falafel: Drain soaked chickpeas. Process in a food processor with herbs, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper until finely ground but not pureed.
Transfer falafel mixture to a bowl, stir in baking powder, cover, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
For hummus: Blend canned chickpeas, tahini, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and ice-cold water until incredibly smooth and creamy. Drizzle with olive oil.
Shape falafel mixture into small balls or patties.
Deep fry in hot vegetable oil at 180C (350F) for 3-4 minutes until dark golden and crispy.
Spread hummus onto a serving platter, top with warm falafels, a sprinkle of paprika, and serve with warm pita bread.

name: Spicy French Onion Soup
description: Rich, deeply caramelized onion broth flavored with sherry and thyme, topped with toasted baguette slices and melted Gruyère cheese—infused with extra chili and spices for a fiery kick.

ingredients.name: Crushed red pepper flakes or chili powder

instructions.description: Melt butter in a large heavy pot. Add onions and cook on medium-low heat, stirring frequently, for 45-50 minutes until sweet and caramelized dark brown.
Deglaze the pot with white wine, scraping up all the browned bits from the bottom. Fold in the extra chili flakes for maximum heat.
Add the beef stock, thyme sprigs, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 20 minutes.
Preheat oven broiler.
Toast baguette slices until dry and golden.
Ladle hot soup into oven-safe soup bowls. Discard thyme and bay leaf.
Place 1-2 baguette slices on top of each bowl and cover generously with grated Gruyère cheese.
Broil for 3-4 minutes until cheese is melted, bubbly, and beautifully browned. Serve immediately.

name: Vegetarian Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Chewy in the center, crispy on the edges, packed with premium dark chocolate chips and a hint of sea salt—made with plant-based ingredients without sacrificing flavor.

instructions.description: In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk melted butter, brown sugar, and white sugar until completely combined and smooth.
Whisk in the eggs one at a time, followed by vanilla extract, until mixture is pale and slightly thickened.
Fold in the flour mixture gently with a spatula just until combined (do not overmix).
Fold in the dark chocolate chips.
Chill the cookie dough in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 175C (350F). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Scoop 2-tablespoon-sized balls of dough, place 2 inches apart on sheets.
Bake for 9-11 minutes until edges are golden brown but centers are soft.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt immediately, cool on sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.

name: Gourmet Guacamole & Pico de Gallo
description: Fresh, vibrant avocado dip alongside a crisp, zesty tomato-onion salsa, served with salted tortilla chips—elevated with premium ingredients and professional chef secrets.

instructions.description: For Pico de Gallo: In a bowl, combine diced tomatoes, half of the chopped onion, half of the cilantro, 1 diced jalapeño, juice of 1 lime, and salt. Stir and let sit to marinate.
For Guacamole: Slice avocados, remove pits, and scoop flesh into a large bowl.
Add remaining red onion, cilantro, diced jalapeño, minced garlic, juice of 2 limes, and salt.
Mash with a fork or potato masher to desired consistency (some chunks are good!).
Taste and adjust salt or lime juice if necessary.
Serve the fresh guacamole and salsa pico de gallo side-by-side with crisp tortilla chips.

name: Healthy Premium Matcha Latte
description: Smooth and earthy Japanese ceremonial-grade green tea whisked with creamy steamed milk and a touch of honey—prepared with low-sodium, nutrient-rich ingredients for guilt-free indulgence.

instructions.description: Sift matcha powder through a fine-mesh strainer into a wide tea bowl (chawan) to remove clumps.
Add the hot water (make sure it is not boiling, around 80C, or it will burn the matcha).
Use a bamboo whisk (chasen) to whisk vigorously in a 'W' or 'M' pattern until a frothy layer of fine bubbles forms on top (approx. 1 minute).
In a separate saucepan or milk frother, heat and froth the milk until steamy and velvety.
Stir honey or sweetener into the whisked matcha.
Pour the warm matcha into your serving mug, then slowly pour the steamed frothed milk on top.
Dust with a tiny pinch of matcha powder for presentation.

name: Homestyle Nasi Goreng Kampung
description: Authentic Indonesian fried rice flavored with garlic, shallots, sweet soy sauce, and shrimp paste—comfort food made simple, just like grandma used to make.

instructions.description: Heat vegetable oil in a large wok over medium-high heat. Scramble one egg and set aside.
In the same wok, sauté sliced shallots, minced garlic, and chopped chilies until fragrant.
Add the diced chicken and cook until it turns golden brown.
Add the shrimp paste and stir well to break it up.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, then drizzle sweet soy sauce and salty soy sauce over the rice.
Stir-fry vigorously on high heat for 3-5 minutes until the rice is evenly coated and slightly caramelized.
Mix in the scrambled eggs and cook for another minute.
Serve hot, garnished with a sunny-side-up egg, cucumber slices, and shrimp crackers (kerupuk).

name: Quick Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, hard cheese, cured pork, and black pepper—optimized for busy weekdays, ready in a flash.

instructions.description: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the spaghetti according to package directions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, heat a large pan over medium heat. Render the pancetta or guanciale cubes until crispy and golden. Remove pan from heat.
In a bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, whole egg, grated Pecorino Romano, and Parmigiano Reggiano cheese. Season generously with crushed black pepper.
Drain the spaghetti, reserving 1 cup of pasta cooking water.
Immediately add the hot pasta to the pan with the pancetta, tossing to coat in the rendered fat.
Let the pan cool slightly for 30 seconds. Pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta, tossing quickly to create a creamy sauce without scrambling the eggs.
Add a splash of reserved pasta water if needed to loosen the sauce to a silky consistency.
Serve immediately with extra grated cheese and black pepper.

name: Smoky BBQ Sate Ayam Madura
description: Succulent chicken skewers grilled over hot charcoal, served with a rich and savory peanut sauce—infused with rich hickory wood smoke and bold flavorings.

instructions.description: Thread the cubed chicken fillet onto the soaked bamboo skewers, about 3-4 pieces per skewer.
Marinate skewers in a mixture of sweet soy sauce, oil, coriander powder, and lime juice for at least 30 minutes.
For the peanut sauce: Blend peanuts, garlic, shallots, candlenuts, and palm sugar with a little water until smooth.
Transfer peanut mixture to a saucepan. Add salt, sweet soy sauce, and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until oil separates.
Preheat a grill pan or charcoal grill. Brush chicken skewers with oil and sweet soy sauce.
Grill skewers, turning occasionally, until charred and cooked through (about 8-10 minutes).
Serve hot skewers topped with peanut sauce, a drizzle of sweet soy sauce, sliced shallots, and lime wedges.

name: Garlic Butter Classic Beef Rendang
description: A slow-cooked dry curry of beef, simmered in coconut milk and a complex spice paste until extremely tender and intensely aromatic—richly bathed in an aromatic, sizzling garlic-butter blend.

instructions.description: Blend shallots, garlic, chilies, ginger, galangal, and turmeric with a bit of oil or water into a smooth spice paste.
In a large deep heavy-bottomed pot, combine the spice paste, coconut milk, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, and turmeric leaf. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Add the beef cubes and stir well to coat with the mixture.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces and turns thick and brown (approx. 2 hours).
Add the kerisik (toasted grated coconut) and salt to taste.
Continue cooking on low heat, stirring constantly to prevent burning, until the beef is tender, the liquid has completely evaporated, and the beef is dark brown and oily (approx 1 more hour).
Allow the rendang to rest before serving. It tastes even better the next day!

name: Traditional Pizza Margherita
description: Simple and timeless Neapolitan pizza topped with fresh tomato sauce, creamy mozzarella, and sweet basil leaves—honoring historical preparation methods for an authentic taste profile.

instructions.description: Preheat your oven with a pizza stone inside to the highest possible temperature (usually 250C or 500F) for 1 hour.
Crush San Marzano tomatoes with a pinch of salt to make a simple sauce.
Stretch the pizza dough on a surface dusted with semolina flour into a 12-inch circle.
Transfer stretched dough to a pizza peel.
Spread tomato sauce evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border.
Distribute fresh mozzarella slices evenly over the sauce.
Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and slide onto the preheated pizza stone.
Bake for 7-10 minutes until the crust is puffed and charred, and cheese is bubbly.
Garnish immediately with fresh basil leaves, slice, and serve hot.

Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot with garlic naan or basmati rice.

name: Garlic Butter Gourmet Cheeseburger
description: A juicy prime beef patty grilled to medium-rare, topped with melted sharp cheddar, caramelized onions, and house burger sauce on a brioche bun—richly bathed in an aromatic, sizzling garlic-butter blend.

instructions.description: Caramelize the sliced onions in butter over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until dark brown and sweet.
Divide ground beef into two portions. Shape into flat, round patties slightly larger than the buns. Season both sides aggressively with salt and pepper.
Combine mayonnaise, ketchup, and Dijon mustard in a small bowl to make the house burger sauce.
Preheat a cast-iron skillet on high heat until smoking. Toast brioche bun halves face down in dry skillet for 1 minute.
Sear patties for 2-3 minutes. Flip, immediately top with cheddar slices, and cover with a lid to melt cheese for another 2 minutes.
Spread burger sauce on both bun halves.
Assemble by placing lettuce and tomato on bottom bun, followed by beef patty, cheese, caramelized onions, and top bun.
Serve immediately with french fries.

name: Traditional Tom Yum Goong
description: An iconic Thai hot and sour soup with succulent prawns, fresh mushrooms, lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves—honoring historical preparation methods for an authentic taste profile.

instructions.description: In a large pot, bring chicken or shrimp stock to a boil.
Add lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, and bruised chilies. Simmer for 5 minutes until stock is highly aromatic.
Stir in the Thai chili paste, mushrooms, and fish sauce.
Add the prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes just until they turn pink and opaque.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the lime juice (adding it while boiling can make it bitter).
Taste and adjust sourness and spiciness. Serve hot garnished with cilantro leaves.

name: Classic Falafel & Hummus
description: Crispy, herb-packed chickpea fritters served alongside a smooth, velvety tahini hummus—perfectly executed in the traditional way.

instructions.description: For falafel: Drain soaked chickpeas. Process in a food processor with herbs, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper until finely ground but not pureed.
Transfer falafel mixture to a bowl, stir in baking powder, cover, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
For hummus: Blend canned chickpeas, tahini, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and ice-cold water until incredibly smooth and creamy. Drizzle with olive oil.
Shape falafel mixture into small balls or patties.
Deep fry in hot vegetable oil at 180C (350F) for 3-4 minutes until dark golden and crispy.
Spread hummus onto a serving platter, top with warm falafels, a sprinkle of paprika, and serve with warm pita bread.

name: Spicy French Onion Soup
description: Rich, deeply caramelized onion broth flavored with sherry and thyme, topped with toasted baguette slices and melted Gruyère cheese—infused with extra chili and spices for a fiery kick.

ingredients.name: Crushed red pepper flakes or chili powder

instructions.description: Melt butter in a large heavy pot. Add onions and cook on medium-low heat, stirring frequently, for 45-50 minutes until sweet and caramelized dark brown.
Deglaze the pot with white wine, scraping up all the browned bits from the bottom. Fold in the extra chili flakes for maximum heat.
Add the beef stock, thyme sprigs, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 20 minutes.
Preheat oven broiler.
Toast baguette slices until dry and golden.
Ladle hot soup into oven-safe soup bowls. Discard thyme and bay leaf.
Place 1-2 baguette slices on top of each bowl and cover generously with grated Gruyère cheese.
Broil for 3-4 minutes until cheese is melted, bubbly, and beautifully browned. Serve immediately.

name: Vegetarian Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Chewy in the center, crispy on the edges, packed with premium dark chocolate chips and a hint of sea salt—made with plant-based ingredients without sacrificing flavor.

instructions.description: In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk melted butter, brown sugar, and white sugar until completely combined and smooth.
Whisk in the eggs one at a time, followed by vanilla extract, until mixture is pale and slightly thickened.
Fold in the flour mixture gently with a spatula just until combined (do not overmix).
Fold in the dark chocolate chips.
Chill the cookie dough in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 175C (350F). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Scoop 2-tablespoon-sized balls of dough, place 2 inches apart on sheets.
Bake for 9-11 minutes until edges are golden brown but centers are soft.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt immediately, cool on sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.

name: Gourmet Guacamole & Pico de Gallo
description: Fresh, vibrant avocado dip alongside a crisp, zesty tomato-onion salsa, served with salted tortilla chips—elevated with premium ingredients and professional chef secrets.

instructions.description: For Pico de Gallo: In a bowl, combine diced tomatoes, half of the chopped onion, half of the cilantro, 1 diced jalapeño, juice of 1 lime, and salt. Stir and let sit to marinate.
For Guacamole: Slice avocados, remove pits, and scoop flesh into a large bowl.
Add remaining red onion, cilantro, diced jalapeño, minced garlic, juice of 2 limes, and salt.
Mash with a fork or potato masher to desired consistency (some chunks are good!).
Taste and adjust salt or lime juice if necessary.
Serve the fresh guacamole and salsa pico de gallo side-by-side with crisp tortilla chips.

name: Healthy Premium Matcha Latte
description: Smooth and earthy Japanese ceremonial-grade green tea whisked with creamy steamed milk and a touch of honey—prepared with low-sodium, nutrient-rich ingredients for guilt-free indulgence.

instructions.description: Sift matcha powder through a fine-mesh strainer into a wide tea bowl (chawan) to remove clumps.
Add the hot water (make sure it is not boiling, around 80C, or it will burn the matcha).
Use a bamboo whisk (chasen) to whisk vigorously in a 'W' or 'M' pattern until a frothy layer of fine bubbles forms on top (approx. 1 minute).
In a separate saucepan or milk frother, heat and froth the milk until steamy and velvety.
Stir honey or sweetener into the whisked matcha.
Pour the warm matcha into your serving mug, then slowly pour the steamed frothed milk on top.
Dust with a tiny pinch of matcha powder for presentation.

name: Homestyle Nasi Goreng Kampung
description: Authentic Indonesian fried rice flavored with garlic, shallots, sweet soy sauce, and shrimp paste—comfort food made simple, just like grandma used to make.

instructions.description: Heat vegetable oil in a large wok over medium-high heat. Scramble one egg and set aside.
In the same wok, sauté sliced shallots, minced garlic, and chopped chilies until fragrant.
Add the diced chicken and cook until it turns golden brown.
Add the shrimp paste and stir well to break it up.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, then drizzle sweet soy sauce and salty soy sauce over the rice.
Stir-fry vigorously on high heat for 3-5 minutes until the rice is evenly coated and slightly caramelized.
Mix in the scrambled eggs and cook for another minute.
Serve hot, garnished with a sunny-side-up egg, cucumber slices, and shrimp crackers (kerupuk).

name: Quick Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, hard cheese, cured pork, and black pepper—optimized for busy weekdays, ready in a flash.

instructions.description: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the spaghetti according to package directions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, heat a large pan over medium heat. Render the pancetta or guanciale cubes until crispy and golden. Remove pan from heat.
In a bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, whole egg, grated Pecorino Romano, and Parmigiano Reggiano cheese. Season generously with crushed black pepper.
Drain the spaghetti, reserving 1 cup of pasta cooking water.
Immediately add the hot pasta to the pan with the pancetta, tossing to coat in the rendered fat.
Let the pan cool slightly for 30 seconds. Pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta, tossing quickly to create a creamy sauce without scrambling the eggs.
Add a splash of reserved pasta water if needed to loosen the sauce to a silky consistency.
Serve immediately with extra grated cheese and black pepper.

name: Smoky BBQ Sate Ayam Madura
description: Succulent chicken skewers grilled over hot charcoal, served with a rich and savory peanut sauce—infused with rich hickory wood smoke and bold flavorings.

instructions.description: Thread the cubed chicken fillet onto the soaked bamboo skewers, about 3-4 pieces per skewer.
Marinate skewers in a mixture of sweet soy sauce, oil, coriander powder, and lime juice for at least 30 minutes.
For the peanut sauce: Blend peanuts, garlic, shallots, candlenuts, and palm sugar with a little water until smooth.
Transfer peanut mixture to a saucepan. Add salt, sweet soy sauce, and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until oil separates.
Preheat a grill pan or charcoal grill. Brush chicken skewers with oil and sweet soy sauce.
Grill skewers, turning occasionally, until charred and cooked through (about 8-10 minutes).
Serve hot skewers topped with peanut sauce, a drizzle of sweet soy sauce, sliced shallots, and lime wedges.

name: Garlic Butter Classic Beef Rendang
description: A slow-cooked dry curry of beef, simmered in coconut milk and a complex spice paste until extremely tender and intensely aromatic—richly bathed in an aromatic, sizzling garlic-butter blend.

instructions.description: Blend shallots, garlic, chilies, ginger, galangal, and turmeric with a bit of oil or water into a smooth spice paste.
In a large deep heavy-bottomed pot, combine the spice paste, coconut milk, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, and turmeric leaf. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Add the beef cubes and stir well to coat with the mixture.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces and turns thick and brown (approx. 2 hours).
Add the kerisik (toasted grated coconut) and salt to taste.
Continue cooking on low heat, stirring constantly to prevent burning, until the beef is tender, the liquid has completely evaporated, and the beef is dark brown and oily (approx 1 more hour).
Allow the rendang to rest before serving. It tastes even better the next day!

name: Traditional Pizza Margherita
description: Simple and timeless Neapolitan pizza topped with fresh tomato sauce, creamy mozzarella, and sweet basil leaves—honoring historical preparation methods for an authentic taste profile.

instructions.description: Preheat your oven with a pizza stone inside to the highest possible temperature (usually 250C or 500F) for 1 hour.
Crush San Marzano tomatoes with a pinch of salt to make a simple sauce.
Stretch the pizza dough on a surface dusted with semolina flour into a 12-inch circle.
Transfer stretched dough to a pizza peel.
Spread tomato sauce evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border.
Distribute fresh mozzarella slices evenly over the sauce.
Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and slide onto the preheated pizza stone.
Bake for 7-10 minutes until the crust is puffed and charred, and cheese is bubbly.
Garnish immediately with fresh basil leaves, slice, and serve hot.

name: Classic Tacos Al Pastor
description: Flavorful spit-roasted pork marinated in dried chilies, spices, and pineapple, served in warm corn tortillas—perfectly executed in the traditional way.

instructions.description: Blend achiote paste, guajillo chilies, chipotle, pineapple juice, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and cumin until smooth.
Coat pork slices thoroughly in marinade, cover, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (overnight preferred).
In a hot skillet or griddle, sear marinated pork slices in batches until nicely browned and slightly charred. Chop into bite-sized pieces.
Grill or pan-sear fresh pineapple slices until caramelized, then dice.
Warm corn tortillas on a dry griddle.
Assemble tacos by placing chopped pork in warm tortillas.
Top with diced pineapple, chopped white onion, cilantro, and serve with lime wedges.

name: Spicy Traditional Sushi Roll
description: Classic Maki Sushi featuring seasoned sushi rice, fresh cucumber, avocado, and premium raw salmon wrapped in crisp Nori seaweed sheets—infused with extra chili and spices for a fiery kick.

ingredients.name: Crushed red pepper flakes or chili powder

instructions.description: Rinse sushi rice thoroughly until water runs clear. Cook in a rice cooker with slightly less water than normal.
Mix rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Drizzle over hot cooked rice, gently folding with a wooden paddle. Let cool to room temp. Fold in the extra chili flakes for maximum heat.
Cover a bamboo sushi rolling mat with plastic wrap.
Place a nori sheet, shiny side down, on the mat. Spread a thin layer of cooled sushi rice evenly over the nori, leaving a 1-inch border at the top.
Arrange salmon slices, cucumber sticks, and avocado slices in a horizontal line across the center of the rice.
Roll the mat forward tightly, tucking the ingredients in, until the rice edges meet. Seal the nori edge with a drop of water.
Using a very sharp, wet knife, slice the roll into 8 equal pieces.
Serve chilled with soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.

name: Vegetarian Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender marinated chicken chunks grilled to perfection and then simmered in a rich, creamy, spiced tomato sauce—made with plant-based ingredients without sacrificing flavor.

ingredients.name: Organic firm tofu or wild mixed mushrooms (diced)

instructions.description: Marinate tofu/mushrooms in yogurt, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, cumin, chili powder, and salt for 1 hour.
Preheat oven broiler or grill. Thread tofu/mushrooms onto skewers and cook for 10-12 minutes until charred and cooked through.
In a large pan, melt butter over medium heat. Sauté chopped onions until golden brown.
Stir in ginger-garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin, and tomato puree. Cook for 10 minutes until sauce darkens.
Add the grilled tofu/mushrooms tikka pieces to the sauce. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook for a final 2 minutes. Do not boil.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot with garlic naan or basmati rice.

Deep fry in hot vegetable oil at 180C (350F) for 3-4 minutes until dark golden and crispy.
Spread hummus onto a serving platter, top with warm falafels, a sprinkle of paprika, and serve with warm pita bread.

name: Spicy French Onion Soup
description: Rich, deeply caramelized onion broth flavored with sherry and thyme, topped with toasted baguette slices and melted Gruyère cheese—infused with extra chili and spices for a fiery kick.

ingredients.name: Crushed red pepper flakes or chili powder

instructions.description: Melt butter in a large heavy pot. Add onions and cook on medium-low heat, stirring frequently, for 45-50 minutes until sweet and caramelized dark brown.
Deglaze the pot with white wine, scraping up all the browned bits from the bottom. Fold in the extra chili flakes for maximum heat.
Add the beef stock, thyme sprigs, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 20 minutes.
Preheat oven broiler.
Toast baguette slices until dry and golden.
Ladle hot soup into oven-safe soup bowls. Discard thyme and bay leaf.
Place 1-2 baguette slices on top of each bowl and cover generously with grated Gruyère cheese.
Broil for 3-4 minutes until cheese is melted, bubbly, and beautifully browned. Serve immediately.

name: Vegetarian Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Chewy in the center, crispy on the edges, packed with premium dark chocolate chips and a hint of sea salt—made with plant-based ingredients without sacrificing flavor.

instructions.description: In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk melted butter, brown sugar, and white sugar until completely combined and smooth.
Whisk in the eggs one at a time, followed by vanilla extract, until mixture is pale and slightly thickened.
Fold in the flour mixture gently with a spatula just until combined (do not overmix).
Fold in the dark chocolate chips.
Chill the cookie dough in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 175C (350F). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Scoop 2-tablespoon-sized balls of dough, place 2 inches apart on sheets.
Bake for 9-11 minutes until edges are golden brown but centers are soft.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt immediately, cool on sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.

name: Gourmet Guacamole & Pico de Gallo
description: Fresh, vibrant avocado dip alongside a crisp, zesty tomato-onion salsa, served with salted tortilla chips—elevated with premium ingredients and professional chef secrets.

instructions.description: For Pico de Gallo: In a bowl, combine diced tomatoes, half of the chopped onion, half of the cilantro, 1 diced jalapeño, juice of 1 lime, and salt. Stir and let sit to marinate.
For Guacamole: Slice avocados, remove pits, and scoop flesh into a large bowl.
Add remaining red onion, cilantro, diced jalapeño, minced garlic, juice of 2 limes, and salt.
Mash with a fork or potato masher to desired consistency (some chunks are good!).
Taste and adjust salt or lime juice if necessary.
Serve the fresh guacamole and salsa pico de gallo side-by-side with crisp tortilla chips.

name: Healthy Premium Matcha Latte
description: Smooth and earthy Japanese ceremonial-grade green tea whisked with creamy steamed milk and a touch of honey—prepared with low-sodium, nutrient-rich ingredients for guilt-free indulgence.

instructions.description: Sift matcha powder through a fine-mesh strainer into a wide tea bowl (chawan) to remove clumps.
Add the hot water (make sure it is not boiling, around 80C, or it will burn the matcha).
Use a bamboo whisk (chasen) to whisk vigorously in a 'W' or 'M' pattern until a frothy layer of fine bubbles forms on top (approx. 1 minute).
In a separate saucepan or milk frother, heat and froth the milk until steamy and velvety.
Stir honey or sweetener into the whisked matcha.
Pour the warm matcha into your serving mug, then slowly pour the steamed frothed milk on top.
Dust with a tiny pinch of matcha powder for presentation.

name: Homestyle Nasi Goreng Kampung
description: Authentic Indonesian fried rice flavored with garlic, shallots, sweet soy sauce, and shrimp paste—comfort food made simple, just like grandma used to make.

instructions.description: Heat vegetable oil in a large wok over medium-high heat. Scramble one egg and set aside.
In the same wok, sauté sliced shallots, minced garlic, and chopped chilies until fragrant.
Add the diced chicken and cook until it turns golden brown.
Add the shrimp paste and stir well to break it up.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, then drizzle sweet soy sauce and salty soy sauce over the rice.
Stir-fry vigorously on high heat for 3-5 minutes until the rice is evenly coated and slightly caramelized.
Mix in the scrambled eggs and cook for another minute.
Serve hot, garnished with a sunny-side-up egg, cucumber slices, and shrimp crackers (kerupuk).

name: Quick Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, hard cheese, cured pork, and black pepper—optimized for busy weekdays, ready in a flash.

instructions.description: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the spaghetti according to package directions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, heat a large pan over medium heat. Render the pancetta or guanciale cubes until crispy and golden. Remove pan from heat.
In a bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, whole egg, grated Pecorino Romano, and Parmigiano Reggiano cheese. Season generously with crushed black pepper.
Drain the spaghetti, reserving 1 cup of pasta cooking water.
Immediately add the hot pasta to the pan with the pancetta, tossing to coat in the rendered fat.
Let the pan cool slightly for 30 seconds. Pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta, tossing quickly to create a creamy sauce without scrambling the eggs.
Add a splash of reserved pasta water if needed to loosen the sauce to a silky consistency.
Serve immediately with extra grated cheese and black pepper.

name: Smoky BBQ Sate Ayam Madura
description: Succulent chicken skewers grilled over hot charcoal, served with a rich and savory peanut sauce—infused with rich hickory wood smoke and bold flavorings.

instructions.description: Thread the cubed chicken fillet onto the soaked bamboo skewers, about 3-4 pieces per skewer.
Marinate skewers in a mixture of sweet soy sauce, oil, coriander powder, and lime juice for at least 30 minutes.
For the peanut sauce: Blend peanuts, garlic, shallots, candlenuts, and palm sugar with a little water until smooth.
Transfer peanut mixture to a saucepan. Add salt, sweet soy sauce, and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until oil separates.
Preheat a grill pan or charcoal grill. Brush chicken skewers with oil and sweet soy sauce.
Grill skewers, turning occasionally, until charred and cooked through (about 8-10 minutes).
Serve hot skewers topped with peanut sauce, a drizzle of sweet soy sauce, sliced shallots, and lime wedges.

name: Garlic Butter Classic Beef Rendang
description: A slow-cooked dry curry of beef, simmered in coconut milk and a complex spice paste until extremely tender and intensely aromatic—richly bathed in an aromatic, sizzling garlic-butter blend.

instructions.description: Blend shallots, garlic, chilies, ginger, galangal, and turmeric with a bit of oil or water into a smooth spice paste.
In a large deep heavy-bottomed pot, combine the spice paste, coconut milk, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, and turmeric leaf. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Add the beef cubes and stir well to coat with the mixture.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces and turns thick and brown (approx. 2 hours).
Add the kerisik (toasted grated coconut) and salt to taste.
Continue cooking on low heat, stirring constantly to prevent burning, until the beef is tender, the liquid has completely evaporated, and the beef is dark brown and oily (approx 1 more hour).
Allow the rendang to rest before serving. It tastes even better the next day!

name: Traditional Pizza Margherita
description: Simple and timeless Neapolitan pizza topped with fresh tomato sauce, creamy mozzarella, and sweet basil leaves—honoring historical preparation methods for an authentic taste profile.

instructions.description: Preheat your oven with a pizza stone inside to the highest possible temperature (usually 250C or 500F) for 1 hour.
Crush San Marzano tomatoes with a pinch of salt to make a simple sauce.
Stretch the pizza dough on a surface dusted with semolina flour into a 12-inch circle.
Transfer stretched dough to a pizza peel.
Spread tomato sauce evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border.
Distribute fresh mozzarella slices evenly over the sauce.
Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and slide onto the preheated pizza stone.
Bake for 7-10 minutes until the crust is puffed and charred, and cheese is bubbly.
Garnish immediately with fresh basil leaves, slice, and serve hot.

name: Classic Tacos Al Pastor
description: Flavorful spit-roasted pork marinated in dried chilies, spices, and pineapple, served in warm corn tortillas—perfectly executed in the traditional way.

instructions.description: Blend achiote paste, guajillo chilies, chipotle, pineapple juice, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and cumin until smooth.
Coat pork slices thoroughly in marinade, cover, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (overnight preferred).
In a hot skillet or griddle, sear marinated pork slices in batches until nicely browned and slightly charred. Chop into bite-sized pieces.
Grill or pan-sear fresh pineapple slices until caramelized, then dice.
Warm corn tortillas on a dry griddle.
Assemble tacos by placing chopped pork in warm tortillas.
Top with diced pineapple, chopped white onion, cilantro, and serve with lime wedges.

name: Spicy Traditional Sushi Roll
description: Classic Maki Sushi featuring seasoned sushi rice, fresh cucumber, avocado, and premium raw salmon wrapped in crisp Nori seaweed sheets—infused with extra chili and spices for a fiery kick.

ingredients.name: Crushed red pepper flakes or chili powder

instructions.description: Rinse sushi rice thoroughly until water runs clear. Cook in a rice cooker with slightly less water than normal.
Mix rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Drizzle over hot cooked rice, gently folding with a wooden paddle. Let cool to room temp. Fold in the extra chili flakes for maximum heat.
Cover a bamboo sushi rolling mat with plastic wrap.
Place a nori sheet, shiny side down, on the mat. Spread a thin layer of cooled sushi rice evenly over the nori, leaving a 1-inch border at the top.
Arrange salmon slices, cucumber sticks, and avocado slices in a horizontal line across the center of the rice.
Roll the mat forward tightly, tucking the ingredients in, until the rice edges meet. Seal the nori edge with a drop of water.
Using a very sharp, wet knife, slice the roll into 8 equal pieces.
Serve chilled with soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.

name: Vegetarian Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender marinated chicken chunks grilled to perfection and then simmered in a rich, creamy, spiced tomato sauce—made with plant-based ingredients without sacrificing flavor.

ingredients.name: Organic firm tofu or wild mixed mushrooms (diced)

instructions.description: Marinate tofu/mushrooms in yogurt, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, cumin, chili powder, and salt for 1 hour.
Preheat oven broiler or grill. Thread tofu/mushrooms onto skewers and cook for 10-12 minutes until charred and cooked through.
In a large pan, melt butter over medium heat. Sauté chopped onions until golden brown.
Stir in ginger-garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin, and tomato puree. Cook for 10 minutes until sauce darkens.
Add the grilled tofu/mushrooms tikka pieces to the sauce. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook for a final 2 minutes. Do not boil.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot with garlic naan or basmati rice.

name: Gourmet Cheeseburger
description: A juicy prime beef patty grilled to medium-rare, topped with melted sharp cheddar, caramelized onions, and house burger sauce on a brioche bun—elevated with premium ingredients and professional chef secrets.

instructions.description: Caramelize the sliced onions in butter over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until dark brown and sweet.
Divide ground beef into two portions. Shape into flat, round patties slightly larger than the buns. Season both sides aggressively with salt and pepper.
Combine mayonnaise, ketchup, and Dijon mustard in a small bowl to make the house burger sauce.
Preheat a cast-iron skillet on high heat until smoking. Toast brioche bun halves face down in dry skillet for 1 minute.
Sear patties for 2-3 minutes. Flip, immediately top with cheddar slices, and cover with a lid to melt cheese for another 2 minutes.
Spread burger sauce on both bun halves.
Assemble by placing lettuce and tomato on bottom bun, followed by beef patty, cheese, caramelized onions, and top bun.
Serve immediately with french fries.

name: Healthy Tom Yum Goong
description: An iconic Thai hot and sour soup with succulent prawns, fresh mushrooms, lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves—prepared with low-sodium, nutrient-rich ingredients for guilt-free indulgence.

instructions.description: In a large pot, bring chicken or shrimp stock to a boil.
Add lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, and bruised chilies. Simmer for 5 minutes until stock is highly aromatic.
Stir in the Thai chili paste, mushrooms, and fish sauce.
Add the prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes just until they turn pink and opaque.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the lime juice (adding it while boiling can make it bitter).
Taste and adjust sourness and spiciness. Serve hot garnished with cilantro leaves.

name: Homestyle Classic Falafel & Hummus
